title: Wholesome Dutch Flour Pancakes
description: Yeasty, light Dutch pancakes infused with cardamom and a deglazing fond, cooked in a copper pot for a Mediterranean touch.
instructions: In a large bowl whisk together flour, yeast, salt and sugar until combined.
Add warm water and milk, stir until a loose batter forms.
Whisk in eggs one at a time, then fold in ground cardamom and lemon zest.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the batter rise at room temperature for 60 minutes.
Heat a copper pot over medium heat and brush it with melted butter.
Pour a small ladleful of batter into the pot to form a thin pancake, rotating to coat the bottom.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface, then flip and cook the other side until golden.
When a pancake is finished, deglaze the pot with a splash of olive oil, scraping up any browned bits to flavor the next batch.
Repeat steps 6-8 until all batter is used, spooning pancakes onto a plate.
Serve warm with a drizzle of honey or fresh fruit.
